purpose: Control points for georeferencing H. F. Walling's 1852 Concord map against modern WGS84 coordinates before accepting precise historical-site locations.

old_map.title: Map of the town of Concord, Middlesex County Mass
old_map.note: Pixel coordinates refer to the local 25% LOC JPEG. They are manual draft picks from visual inspection, not a finished georectification.

control_points.label: Concord village center / Town House vicinity
control_points.modern_source: Existing atlas Concord Town House candidate; still needs archival confirmation for Lyceum/Town House identity.
control_points.note: Useful town-center anchor, but refine against the village inset or a named building before using for house-level locations.

control_points.label: Walden Pond center
control_points.modern_source: OpenStreetMap/Nominatim Walden Pond candidate cached in the atlas.
control_points.note: Stable natural-feature control point; use pond outline, not the label, when refining.

control_points.label: White Pond center
control_points.note: Stable natural-feature control point; useful because it is far from Walden and the village center.

control_points.label: Old North Bridge
control_points.modern_source: OpenStreetMap/Nominatim Old North Bridge candidate, checked 2026-07-02.
control_points.note: Additional check point north of the village. The bridge point is harder to read on the 25% image and should be refined from a higher-resolution image.

next_steps: Refine each old-map pixel against a higher-resolution crop and record the exact landmark used.
Use at least three non-collinear accepted control points to compute an affine or thin-plate-spline transform.
Check residual error before using the transform for Sanborn's house, Thoreau's Main Street house, Lyceum/Town House scenes, or other house-level claims.
